tool.name: nthseqset
tool.summary: Select one alignment set by 1-based ordinal position from a multi-alignment input

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `nthseqset` selects one alignment set from an input that may contain more than one multiple alignment. Epithema v1 supports deterministic 1-based selection from Stockholm files containing multiple alignments and also accepts a single aligned FASTA or Stockholm input as set `1`.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local alignment input path plus one selection number. The selection number is **1-based** and must refer to an existing alignment set after the shared loader has parsed the input.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits the selected alignment through the shared alignment result path. CLI summaries report the input path, selected set number, total set count, and Stockholm output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema nthseqset`. Validation currently covers deterministic selection of the second alignment from a committed multi-Stockholm fixture, single-alignment passthrough as set `1`, and out-of-range failure behavior.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports ordinal set selection only. It does not support identifier-based alignment selection, combined reporting across sets, or grouped filtering within one invocation.

artifacts.label: Two-alignment Stockholm fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed Stockholm fixture containing two distinct alignment sets for deterministic ordinal selection.

examples.title: Select the second alignment set from a multi-Stockholm input
examples.description: Runs `nthseqset` against a committed Stockholm fixture containing two alignments and returns only the second set.

expected_outputs.label: Selected alignment as Stockholm
expected_outputs.description: A Stockholm alignment containing the second parsed set, whose first row accession is `gamma`.
